title: calophyllum pentapetalum var. pentapetalum
intro: Calophyllum pentapetalum var. pentapetalum, also known as the Burmese canary or Indian walnut, is a plant species in the family Calophyllaceae that is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as calophyllolide and isocalophyllolide. The health benefits of Calophyllum pentapetalum var. pentapetalum include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and insect repellent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
